summary: Merged matching pass focused on ifcoget plus scattered permuter-driven fixes across Kirby, stage, menu, game-mode, and baselib code. The automated decomp.dev report recorded +5580 matched bytes, 22 new 100% matches, and 5 remaining ifcoget improvements; the slice contains no human review comments.
key_files: src/melee/if/ifcoget.c - main target; data layout, archive string storage, text object state, PAD_STACK, goto/control-flow, and SisLib call fixes
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c - Kirby copy-ability matches using inline dat_attrs access, HSD_GObjGetUserData, fp2 temporaries, and PAD_STACK
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN.c - corrected motion state enum choices to ftKb_MS_Eat and ftKb_MS_EatAir
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd.c - Zelda-copy Kirby matches using HSD_GObjGetUserData temporaries and PAD_STACK
src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c, src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c, src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.h, src/melee/gm/types.h - save-record score field x7E changed from s16 to u16 and pointer math replaced with typed access
src/melee/gr/grrcruise.c, src/melee/gr/grrcruise.h, src/melee/gr/types.h - CollData signature and grRCruise_Entry layout introduced to replace raw byte/pointer arithmetic
src/melee/gr/grcastle.c - grCastleParams padded to x12C flexible s16 array and used for typed lookup
src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.h - HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 prototype changed to HSD_Text*, s32 entry_idx, char* fmt, ...
src/melee/if/textdraw.c - DevText_Remove linked-list removal reshaped with permuter temporaries
src/melee/mn/mnmain.c - main-menu matches via stack/temp/permuter slop changes
src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c - hsd_8039CEAC loop rewritten as a for loop for matching
what_changed: ifcoget globals were reshaped: un_803F9E08 now stores the archive strings "IfCoGet.dat" and "ScInfCgt_scene_data", and un_804A1F58 was named as struct un_804A1F58_t with x4 narrowed to unsigned char and array length changed to 7.

Several ifcoget functions were adjusted for matching: fn_802FED14 uses HSD_GObjGetHSDObj, un_802FEFAC uses comma-expression wrappers around HSD_CObjLoadDesc/lb_80011AC4, fn_802FF218 uses a goto label and typed HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 calls, and un_802FF364/un_802FF498/un_802FF570/un_802FF620 add PAD_STACK, base pointers, fixed memzero(0x80), and flag writes.

Kirby SpecialN functions gained small but match-critical changes: ftKb_SpecialN_800F6388 uses ftKb_MS_Eat instead of ftKb_MS_EatWait, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F63EC uses ftKb_MS_EatAir instead of ftKb_MS_EatFall.
Kirby Pikachu/Samus/Zelda copy-special code added inline getter helpers, HSD_GObjGetUserData paths, PAD_STACK calls, and extra temporaries such as fp2/new_var to satisfy register allocation.

gm_80163298 was simplified from raw save-data pointer arithmetic to typed gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x114[index] access, and gmMainLib_8015D7EC plus FighterData.x7E were changed from signed to unsigned 16-bit record types.

Stage code replaced raw layout arithmetic with partial structs: grCastleParams now includes padding to x12C and a flexible s16 x12C array, while grRCruise_Entry and grRCruise_GroundVars.entries model the 0x18-byte entry array used by fn_802010A4.
fn_802010A4 now takes CollData* instead of u8* and reads coll->x34_flags.b1234 rather than manually shifting arg2[0x34].

SisLib text formatting was made more explicit: HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 now has an entry_idx and char* fmt prototype, and callers in gm_18A5.c, mncharsel.c, and ifcoget.c pass char* buffers or literal formats like "%d" and "".

Menu and interface helper functions received mechanical matching changes: DevText_Remove uses extra linked-list temporaries, mn_80229F60 caches mn_8022F298 results in a float, and mn_8022BA1C caches HSD_PadCopyStatus.
hsd_8039CEAC in particle.c changed from a do/while loop to a for loop over 16 bins, matching the same logic with a different control-flow shape.
smart_moves: The PR paired local permuter fixes with real type/header improvements, so some matches became cleaner rather than only adding slop.
Changing FighterData.x7E and gmMainLib_8015D7EC to u16 across the struct, implementation, header, and caller let gm_80163298 express score comparisons naturally and remove a noted pointer-math TODO.
Introducing grRCruise_Entry and entries at the documented offset converted fn_802010A4 from byte arithmetic into searchable field accesses while preserving the match.
The ifcoget data fix stored archive names inside the static data struct, which helped both code and data-section matching instead of leaving duplicated string literals in the loader call.
Workarounds were clearly labeled with TODO or 'Permuter slop' comments, making future cleanup targets identifiable.
Updating HSD_SisLib_803A70A0's varargs prototype forced callers to encode whether they pass a format string, empty string, or char buffer, reducing ambiguous void*/u8* casts.
decomp_lessons: A one-byte or few-byte mismatch can be caused by enum choice, signedness, or call prototype rather than the surrounding algorithm.
When GET_FIGHTER or GET_GROUND macros produce the wrong codegen, a tiny inline getter or an explicit HSD_GObjGetUserData call can be a useful temporary matching tactic.
Use offset-annotated partial structs to replace raw pointer math only after the layout is plausible; grCastleParams.x12C and grRCruise_Entry show this pattern.
For varargs functions, the declared parameter types and literal format string can affect both readability and matching; avoid leaving text APIs as untyped ellipses when evidence supports a prototype.
PAD_STACK, redundant temporaries, comma expressions, and harmless self-assignments are acceptable as documented match aids, but should remain searchable cleanup candidates.
Header, struct, and implementation changes should move together; this PR updated gm and gr headers alongside call sites to keep type evidence consistent.
naming_conventions: Keep unknown globals/functions under existing un_/fn_ address-style names when semantics are still weak, e.g. un_804A1F58_t and fn_802FF218.
Use offset-style placeholder fields for partially understood structs, e.g. x7E, x12C, x34_flags.b1234, grRCruise_Entry.x02, x04, x08, x14.
Name partial stage structs by subsystem when known: grCastleParams, grRCruise_Entry, and grRCruise_GroundVars.
Prefer unsigned types for score/record fields when call sites compare against u16 values, as with FighterData.x7E and gmMainLib_8015D7EC.
Use explicit text format literals for SisLib calls where evidence supports them, e.g. "%d" for numeric display and "" for blank text.
Temporary match-only variables named new_var/new_var2/new_var3 were explicitly labeled 'Permuter slop' and should not be treated as final semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(8) or PAD_STACK(0x10) in several functions to match stack frame shape.
Introduced inline accessors such as ftKb_KpSpecialN_Anim_inline, grMaterial_801C9604_inline, and fn_802010A4_inline when direct field access or macros did not match.
Used explicit HSD_GObjGetUserData/HSD_GObjGetHSDObj calls instead of direct gobj fields or GET_* macros where that matched codegen.
Added extra temporaries such as fp2, new_var, new_var2, and new_var3 to force register allocation/evaluation order.
Used goto _done in fn_802FF218 to match loop exit/control flow instead of initializing y to -1 before the loop.
Used comma expressions like (0, HSD_CObjLoadDesc(...)) and (0, lb_80011AC4(...)) in ifcoget setup calls as codegen nudges.
Kept memzero(un_804A1F58, 0x80) rather than sizeof(un_804A1F58), with a TODO noting the desired future size fix.
Converted hsd_8039CEAC from do/while to for-loop form without changing the 16-iteration bin traversal logic.
Replaced raw shifts and pointer arithmetic with typed fields only where they still matched, such as CollData.x34_flags.b1234 and grRCruise_Entry accesses.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
The automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 recorded matched code increasing to 57.52%, with +0.14% and +5580 bytes.

The automated report listed 22 new 100% matches, including ifcoget un_802FF364/un_802FEFAC/fn_802FED14/un_802FF1B4/un_802FF498, Kirby SpecialN variants, gm_80163298, grCastle_801CE7E8, grMaterial_801C9604, fn_802010A4, DevText_Remove, mn_80229F60, mn_8022BA1C, fn_801D3084, and others.
The automated report also listed 5 unmatched-item improvements, all in ifcoget: .data, un_802FF570, un_802FF620, fn_802FF218, and .sdata.
follow_up_queries: grCastleParams x12C flexible array size TODO
GET_GROUND inline user_data did not work grMaterial_801C9604 fn_802010A4
